page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores utilizing the Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-to-core ratio, each of the 64 vCPUs maps directly to a physical core, eliminating resource contention from hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local NVMe or SSD storage, requiring reliance on external block storage volumes for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps. The lack of integrated local storage represents a tradeoff, as disk I/O performance will depend entirely on network-attached storage configurations. However, the dedicated CPU allocation and high memory capacity offer stable, predictable performance for memory-intensive workloads.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon cores, the ecs.hfr6.16xlarge is designed for memory-intensive applications. It is highly suitable for enterprise databases, in-memory caching layers, large-scale data processing, and enterprise resource planning systems. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core model and high memory density provide a stable platform for workloads that cannot tolerate the performance jitter associated with shared-core or hyperthreaded environments.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at 3.1 GHz, with a single thread per core configuration that ensures dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage operations must utilize network-attached block storage, supported by a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. This hardware profile is engineered for memory-heavy workloads, making it highly suitable for in-memory databases, large-scale data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring consistent, non-shared compute resources.
